title: Ebola Virus Sequencing Protocol
author: Josh Quick
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7nwhmfe

steps.instruction: Mix the following components in an 0.2mL 8-strip tube; Component Volume 50ยตM random hexamers 1 ยตL 10mM dNTPs mix (10mM each) 1 ยตL Template RNA 10 ยตL Total 12 ยตL Viral RNA input from a clinical sample should be between Ct 18-35. If Ct is between 12-15, then dilute the sample 100-fold in water, if between 15-18 then dilute 10-fold in water. This will reduce the likelihood of PCR-inhibition.
Gently mix by pipetting and pulse spin the tube to collect liquid at the bottom of the tube.
Incubate the reaction as follows: 65 ยฐC for 00:05:00 Place on ice for 00:01:00
Add the following to the annealed template RNA: Component Volume SSIV Buffer 4 ยตL 100mM DTT 1 ยตL RNaseOUT RNase Inhibitor 1 ยตL SSIV Reverse Transcriptase 1 ยตL Total 20 ยตL
Gently mix by pipetting and pulse spin the tube to collect liquid at the bottom of the tube.
Incubate the reaction as follows: 42 ยฐC 01:30:00 70 ยฐC 00:10:00 Hold at 5 ยฐC

If required resuspend lyophilised primers at a concentration of 100ยตM each Ebola V2 primers for this protocol were designed using Primal Scheme and generate overlapping 400nt amplicons. Primer names and dilutions are listed in the table below.

Generate primer pool stocks by adding 5 ยตL of each primer pair to a 1.5 mL Eppendorf labelled either โPool 1 (100ยตM)โ or โPool 2 (100ยตM)โ. Total volume should be 505 ยตL for Pool 1 (100ยตM) and 530 ยตL for Pool 2 (100ยตM). These are your 100ยตM stocks of each primer pool. Primers should be prepped and aliquoted prior to departure in a sterile PCR cabinet. At no stage should primers or PCR reagents be anywhere near the template until cDNA addition.

Dilute this primer pool 1:10 in molecular grade water, to generate 10ยตM primer stocks. It is recommend that multiple aliquots of each primer pool are made to in case of degradation or contamination. %s %s Ebov-10-Pan_61_RIGHT_alt2AGGTATTTCTGATTTTACAGTCATGCC1100ยตMEbov-10-Pan_62_LEFTCCTGTCAGATGGAATAGTGTTTTGGTEbov-10-Pan_62_RIGHTAATTTTTGTGTGCGACCATTTTTCC2100ยตM Primers need to be used at a final concentration of 0.015ยตM per primer. In this case, Pool 1 has 101 primers in it so the requirement is 3.8ยตL of Pool 1 (10ยตM) primers per 25ยตL reaction. Pool 2 has 106 primers the requirement is 4.0ยตL of Pool 2 (10ยตM) primers per 25ยตL reaction. For other schemes, adjust the volume added appropriately.

In the mastermix hood set up the multiplex PCR reactions as follows in 0.2mL 8-strip PCR tubes: Component Pool 1 Pool 2 NEB Q5 Polymerase 2X MasterMix 12.5 ยตL 12.5 ยตL Primer Pool 1 or 2 (10ยตM) 3.8 ยตL 4.0 ยตL Water 6.2 ยตL 6.0 ยตL Total 22.5 ยตL 22.5 ยตL The mastermix hood should prepared by sterilising with UV and treated with MediPal wipes, DNAway and RNAseZap reagents. Wipe down the hood with each sequentially, allowing 5 minutes for drying between each. Pipettes should also be treated in the same way, and UV treated for 30 mins between library preparations. This step should be carried out in the mastermix hood and template should not be taken anywhere near the mastermix hood at any stage.

In the template hood add 2.5 ยตL cDNA to each tube and mix well by pipetting. The template hood should prepared by sterilising with UV and treated with MediPal wipes, DNAway and RNAseZap reagents. Wipe down the hood with each sequentially, allowing 5 minutes for drying between each. Pipettes should also be treated in the same way, and UV treated for 30 mins between library preparations.
Pulse centrifuge the tubes to collect the contents at the bottom of the tube.

Set-up the following program on the thermal cycler: Step Temperature Time Cycles Heat Activation 98 ยฐC 00:00:30 1 Denaturation 98 ยฐC 00:00:15 25-35 Annealing 65 ยฐC 00:05:00 25-35 Hold 4 ยฐC Indefinite 1 Cycle number should be 25 for Ct 18-21 up to a maximum of 35 cycles for Ct 35
Combine the entire contents of โPool 1โ and โPool 2โ PCR reactions for each biological sample into to a single 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.
Quantify the amplicon pools using the Quantus Fluorometer using the ONE dsDNA assay. DNA quantification using the Quantus fluorometer by Josh Quick

Label a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube for each sample. This is a โone-pot ligationโ protocol for native barcoded ligation libraries. We have seen no reduction in performance compared to standard libraries, and is made faster by using the Ultra IIยฎ ligation module which is compatible with the Ultra IIยฎ end repair/dA-tailing module removing a clean-up step.

Normalise the input but adding 10 ng amplicon pools to each tube and diluting to 10 ยตL to have an input concentration of 1 ng/ยตL Quantity of amplicons can vary from 10-50ng depending on the amplicon length, any more than this and the molarity of DNA ends will be too high for efficient barcoding. You need to have 6 samples per native barcoded library to have sufficient material at the end.
Barcode the amplicon pools using native barcodes. One-pot native barcoding of amplicons by Josh Quick

Quantify the final library using the Quantus Fluorometer using the ONE dsDNA assay. DNA quantification using the Quantus fluorometer by Josh Quick Final library can be now be stored in EB at 4ยฐC for up to a week if needed otherwise proceed directly to MinION sequencing.
Start the sequencing run using MinKNOW. Starting a MinION sequencing run using MinKNOW by Josh Quick
Clean-up the amplicons using the following protocol: Amplicon clean-up using SPRI beads by Josh Quick
Prime the flowcell and load 30 ng sequencing library onto the flowcell. Priming and loading a MinION flowcell by Josh Quick From experience we know 30 ng is optimum loading input for short amplicons.
